structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Operation in Unoccupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The supply fan runs whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Supply fan shall run
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.

evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed Ramp Limitation
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan speed changes are limited to no more than 10% per minute via a ramp function.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Limit fan speed changes to ≤10% per minute
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Mode Fan Speed and SAT Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with heating coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In heating mode, fan speed and SAT setpoint are controlled based on heating loop signal: fan speed resets from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed as signal goes from 100% to 50%, then stays at MinHeatSpeed from 50% to 0%; SAT setpoint is Heat_SAT from 100% to 50%, then resets from Heat_SAT to zone heating setpoint from 50% to 0%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating and there is a heating coil
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: For heating-loop signal 100% to 50%, FANsp resets from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed; for 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. For heating-loop signal 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT; for 50% to 0%, SATsp resets from Heat_SAT to active zone heating setpoint. Modulate heating coil with PID to maintain SATsp. Cooling coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If there is a heating coil, when Zone State is Heating 1. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed. 2. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. 3. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT. 4. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint. 5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Cooling coil OFF

evidence_quote: If there is a heating coil, when Zone State is Heating 1. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed. 2. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. 3. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT. 4. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint. 5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Cooling coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Fan Speed and Coil Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband mode, fan speed is set to DeadbandSpeed; if DeadbandSpeed is zero, fan shuts off. Both cooling and heating coils are OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set FANsp to DeadbandSpeed; if DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut fan off. Cooling coil OFF. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When Zone State is Deadband 1. FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off. 2. Cooling coil OFF 3. Heating coil OFF

evidence_quote: When Zone State is Deadband 1. FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off. 2. Cooling coil OFF 3. Heating coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Fan Speed and SAT Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with cooling coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling mode, fan speed and SAT setpoint are controlled based on cooling loop signal: fan speed is MinCoolSpeed from 0% to 50%, then resets from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed from 50% to 100%; SAT setpoint resets from zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT from 0% to 50%, then is Cool_SAT from 50% to 100%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Cooling and there is a cooling coil
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: For cooling-loop signal 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed; for 50% to 100%, FANsp resets from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. For cooling-loop signal 0% to 50%, SATsp resets from active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT; for 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. Modulate cooling coil with PID to maintain SATsp. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling 1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed. 2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. 3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. 4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. 5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Heating coil OFF

evidence_quote: If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling 1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed. 2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. 3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. 4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. 5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Heating coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours; reset interval counter on acknowledgment.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Fan operated for more than 1500 hours
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Mismatch Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarm when fan status differs from command for 15 seconds; Level 2 if commanded on but off, Level 4 if commanded off but on.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status different from command for 15 seconds
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4

structured_payload_candidate.title: Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarm when filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 minutes and fan speed >20% of MaxCoolSpeed. Alarm limit varies with fan speed as DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with fan speed as follows: 𝐷𝑃𝑥=𝐷𝑃100(𝑥)1.4

evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with fan speed as follows: 𝐷𝑃𝑥=𝐷𝑃100(𝑥)1.4

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Operating State Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The operating state of each FCU is defined by commanded positions of heating and cooling valves per Table 5.20.6.2: OS#1 (Heating) when heating valve >0 and cooling valve =0; OS#2 (No Heating or Cooling) when both =0; OS#3 (Cooling) when heating =0 and cooling >0; OS#4 (Unknown) otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Define OS per valve positions
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.

evidence_quote: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Required Points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The following points must be available to AFDD routines: SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC, CC, FS.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Provide SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC, CC, FS to AFDD
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. SAT = supply air temperature b. RAT = return air temperature (if present) c. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint d. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100% e. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100% f. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%

evidence_quote: The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. SAT = supply air temperature b. RAT = return air temperature (if present) c. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint d. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100% e. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100% f. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Rolling Averages Calculation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT, RAT (if fitted), and number of OS changes in previous 60 minutes must be continuously calculated. Operator can adjust averaging window and sampling period.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate 5-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling for SAT_avg, RAT_avg, and ΔOS (number of OS changes in previous 60 minutes)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following values must be continuously calculated by the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently. 1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature 2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature (if fitted) 3. ΔOS = number of changes in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)

evidence_quote: The following values must be continuously calculated by the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently. 1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature 2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature (if fitted) 3. ΔOS = number of changes in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Internal Variables Default Values
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Internal variables for AFDD with default values: ΔTSF=0.5°C, εSAT=1°C, εRAT=1°C, ΔOSmax=7, ModeDelay=30 min, AlarmDelay=30 min, TestModeDelay=120 min. All adjustable by operator.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Table 5.20.6.5 FCU Internal Variables: ΔTSF Temperature rise across supply fan 0.5°C (1°F); εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor 1°C (2°F); εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor 1°C (2°F); ΔOSmax Maximum number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes (moving window) 7; ModeDelay Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in mode 30; AlarmDelay Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm 30; TestModeDelay Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled 120.

evidence_quote: Table 5.20.6.5 FCU Internal Variables: ΔTSF Temperature rise across supply fan 0.5°C (1°F); εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor 1°C (2°F); εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor 1°C (2°F); ΔOSmax Maximum number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes (moving window) 7; ModeDelay Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in mode 30; AlarmDelay Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm 30; TestModeDelay Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled 120.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FC#1: Too Many OS Changes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when ΔOS > ΔOSmax, indicating unstable control or mechanical problem.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #1 Equation ΔOS > ΔOSMAX Applies to OS #1 – #4 Description Too many changes in Operating State Possible Diagnosis Unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem

evidence_quote: FC #1 Equation ΔOS > ΔOSMAX Applies to OS #1 – #4 Description Too many changes in Operating State Possible Diagnosis Unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem

structured_payload_candidate.title: FC#2: SAT Too Low in Full Heating
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when SAT_avg < SATsp - εSAT and HC ≥ 99% in OS#1, indicating sensor error, valve issues, or heating supply problem.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #2 Equation SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized heating coil HW temperature too low or HW unavailable Gas or electric heat is unavailable DX cooling is stuck on

evidence_quote: FC #2 Equation SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized heating coil HW temperature too low or HW unavailable Gas or electric heat is unavailable DX cooling is stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: FC#3: SAT Too High in Full Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when SAT_avg > SATsp + εSAT and CC ≥ 99% in OS#3, indicating sensor error, valve issues, or cooling supply problem.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #3 Equation SAT AVG > SAT SP + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3 Description SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized cooling coil CHW temperature too high or CHW unavailable DX cooling unavailable Gas or electric heat stuck on Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open

evidence_quote: FC #3 Equation SAT AVG > SAT SP + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3 Description SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure Fouled or undersized cooling coil CHW temperature too high or CHW unavailable DX cooling unavailable Gas or electric heat stuck on Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open

structured_payload_candidate.title: FC#4: Temperature Drop Across Inactive Cooling Coil
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with RAT (cooling only)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when SAT_avg - RAT ≥ sqrt(εRAT^2 + εSAT^2) + ΔTSF in OS#2 for cooling-only FCU with RAT, indicating valve leakage or stuck DX cooling.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#4 Equation SAT AVG – RAT ≥ √ƐRAT2+ ƐSAT2 + ΔTSF Applies to OS #2 (cooling only FCU with RAT) Description Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error SAT sensor error Cooling coil valve stuck open or leaking DX cooling stuck on

evidence_quote: FC#4 Equation SAT AVG – RAT ≥ √ƐRAT2+ ƐSAT2 + ΔTSF Applies to OS #2 (cooling only FCU with RAT) Description Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error SAT sensor error Cooling coil valve stuck open or leaking DX cooling stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: FC#5: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with RAT (heating only)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition when SAT_avg - RAT ≤ -sqrt(εRAT^2 + εSAT^2) - ΔTSF in OS#2 for heating-only FCU with RAT, indicating valve leakage or stuck heat.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#5 Equation SAT AVG - RAT ≤ √ƐRAT2+ ƐSAT2 + ΔTSF Applies to OS #2 (heating only FCU with RAT) Description Temperature rise across inactive heating coil Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error SAT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking Gas or electric heat stuck on

evidence_quote: FC#5 Equation SAT AVG - RAT ≤ √ƐRAT2+ ƐSAT2 + ΔTSF Applies to OS #2 (heating only FCU with RAT) Description Temperature rise across inactive heating coil Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error SAT sensor error Heating coil valve stuck open or leaking Gas or electric heat stuck on

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition Evaluation by OS
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Subset of fault conditions evaluated depends on OS: OS#1 evaluates FC#1 and FC#2; OS#2 evaluates FC#5 (heating only) or FC#4 (cooling only); OS#3 evaluates FC#1 and FC#3; OS#4 evaluates FC#1 only.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Evaluate fault conditions per OS as specified
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. In OS#1 (Heating), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS 2. FC#2: SAT too low in full heating b. In OS#2 (Deadband), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#5: Temperature drop across inactive heating coil (heating only FCU 2. FC#4: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil (cooling only FCU c. In OS#3 (Cooling), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS 2. FC#3: SAT too high in full cooling d. In OS#4 (other), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS

evidence_quote: a. In OS#1 (Heating), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS 2. FC#2: SAT too low in full heating b. In OS#2 (Deadband), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#5: Temperature drop across inactive heating coil (heating only FCU 2. FC#4: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil (cooling only FCU c. In OS#3 (Cooling), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS 2. FC#3: SAT too high in full cooling d. In OS#4 (other), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated: 1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Alarm Suppression per FCU
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operator can suppress the alarm for any fault condition for each FCU.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Allow operator to suppress alarm for any fault condition per FCU
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For each FCU, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.

evidence_quote: For each FCU, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Suspension Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions is suspended when FCU is not operating, and for ModeDelay minutes after a mode change of any Zone Group served by the FCU.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: FCU not operating or mode change of Zone Group
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suspend fault condition evaluation
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When FCU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the FCU.

evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When FCU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the FCU.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Non-Applicable Fault Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault conditions not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Do not evaluate fault conditions not applicable to current OS
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.

evidence_quote: Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Alarm Delay Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition must persist continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before being reported to the operator.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Wait AlarmDelay minutes before reporting
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Test Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Test mode sets ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Alarm Level and Content
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a fault condition is reported, it shall be a Level 3 alarm including description and possible diagnoses from Table 5.20.6.6.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.20.6.6.

evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.20.6.6.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Testing/Commissioning Overrides for Valves
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide software switches to force HW valve full open/closed and CHW valve full open/closed, interlocked at plant level.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify software switches can force HW valve full open, HW valve full closed, CHW valve full open, CHW valve full closed, interlocked at plant level.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Provide software switches that interlock to a CHW and hot-water plant level to a. force HW valve full open if there is a hot-water coil, b. force HW valve full closed if there is a hot-water coil, c. force CHW valve full open if there is a CHW coil, and d. force CHW valve full closed if there is a CHW coil.

evidence_quote: Provide software switches that interlock to a CHW and hot-water plant level to a. force HW valve full open if there is a hot-water coil, b. force HW valve full closed if there is a hot-water coil, c. force CHW valve full open if there is a CHW coil, and d. force CHW valve full closed if there is a CHW coil.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chilled-Water Reset Requests Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: All chilled-water reset requests are suppressed (send 0) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress all CHW reset requests (send 0)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.

evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chilled-Water Reset Requests Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send 3 requests if SAT > setpoint by 10°F for 5 min; else send 2 requests if SAT > setpoint by 5°F for 5 min; else send 1 request if CHW valve >95% until <85%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send requests based on SAT deviation and valve position
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests, c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests, d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests, c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests, d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Plant Request Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send 1 chiller plant request if CHW valve >95% until <10%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 1 request if CHW valve >95% until <10%; else 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: a. If the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hot-Water Reset Requests Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: All hot-water reset requests are suppressed (send 0) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress all HW reset requests (send 0)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.

evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hot-Water Reset Requests Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send 3 requests if SAT < setpoint by 30°F for 5 min; else send 2 requests if SAT < setpoint by 15°F for 5 min; else send 1 request if HW valve >95% until <85%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send requests based on SAT deviation and valve position
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 17°C (30°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 3 requests. c. Else if the supply air temperature is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. d. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 17°C (30°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 3 requests. c. Else if the supply air temperature is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. d. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Hot-Water Plant Request Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send 1 heating plant request if HW valve >95% until <10%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 1 request if HW valve >95% until <10%; else 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
